# Personal Business Operating Procedure (TS Model)

## Purpose
This document defines how I operate, communicate, and improve across all areas of my personal business.  
The goal is to enforce discipline, clarity, and consistent progress through structured feedback and accountability.

---

## 1. Communication Discipline

### 1.1 Standard
- All communication must be clear, structured, and intentional.
- Avoid rushed, fragmented, or unclear wording.

### 1.2 Error Correction (Strict)
All mistakes must be identified and corrected:

- Spelling errors
- Grammar issues
- Incomplete or unclear phrasing

#### Example:
> Original: "can i get this better worded"
- Issues:
  - Missing capitalization
  - Informal phrasing
- Corrected:
  - "Can this be rewritten more clearly?"

---

### 1.3 Rule
- Do not ignore small mistakes.
- Small errors compound into unclear thinking.

---

## 2. Feedback System

### 2.1 Expectations
- Feedback must be:
  - Direct
  - Specific
  - Actionable

### 2.2 Correction Format
Every correction should include:
1. What is wrong  
2. Why it is wrong  
3. The corrected version  

---

## 3. Time & Schedule Accountability

### 3.1 Core Rule
If a schedule or plan exists, it must be followed.

### 3.2 Self-Check Trigger
At any point during work, ask:

> “Am I doing what I planned to be doing right now?”

If not:
- Identify the deviation
- Correct immediately or consciously reschedule

---

### 3.3 No Drift Rule
- Unplanned task switching is not allowed without acknowledgment
- “Just checking something quickly” is considered drift

---

## 4. Work Execution

### 4.1 Single Focus Rule
- Only one primary objective per work session

### 4.2 Session Structure
Each session must have:
- Defined goal
- Clear stopping point
- Measurable output

---

## 5. Build vs Improve Discipline

### 5.1 Priority Order
1. Stabilize what exists  
2. Simplify where possible  
3. Improve effectiveness  
4. Only then expand  

### 5.2 Rule
- Do not add new systems to avoid improving existing ones

---

## 6. Financial Awareness

### 6.1 Standard
- Money must be tracked and understood, not just recorded

### 6.2 Expectation
Regularly review:
- Income trends
- Spending patterns
- Value of work performed

---

## 7. Knowledge & Input Control

### 7.1 Consumption Rule
- Information (books, notes, content) must be:
  - Organized
  - Revisited
  - Applied

### 7.2 No Passive Intake
- Do not consume content without extracting value

---

## 8. Daily Operating Loop

### Start of Day
- Define the primary objective
- Confirm alignment with previous plan

### During Work
- Maintain focus
- Check for drift

### End of Day
- Review output
- Identify mistakes or deviations
- Set next priority

---

## 9. Standard of Behavior

- Be precise, not vague  
- Be consistent, not reactive  
- Be structured, not scattered  

---

## Final Principle

Progress is not based on effort alone.

It is based on:
- correction of mistakes
- adherence to structure
- and consistent execution over time
